risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain at High level, creating persistent constraints on project implementation timelines and institutional capacity for executing development activities in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High High

risk_summary: Macroeconomic risks rated High are affecting the project's ability to sustain implementation and achieve development objectives in the fragile context of Pakistan.

risk_title: Refugee Population Pressures on Education Service Delivery
risk_summary: Afghan refugee children are benefiting from project interventions, indicating displacement pressures affecting education service delivery in the project area. Current enrollment of 7,835 refugee children with target of 12,000 reflects ongoing refugee presence straining education systems.
evidence_quote: Refugee children benefiting from direct interventions to enhance learning (Number) Comments on achieving targets In progress. Figure will be updated after new enrollment figures for 2025-26 school year are available. Indicator measures number of Afghan refugee children benefiting from ALPs, GCS, DSS, and ECE

risk_summary: Flood relief and rehabilitation component indicates recent conflict/disaster-related displacement affecting 12 districts. Ongoing reconstruction and rehabilitation works in 900+ schools and 35 fully damaged schools demonstrate significant infrastructure damage from flooding impacting service delivery.

risk_title: Declining Participation in Community-Based Education Programs
risk_summary: Girls' Community Schools (GCS) enrollment shows declining number of active schools (387 to 382) despite increased enrollment, suggesting potential security or access concerns affecting program sustainability in fragile areas.
evidence_quote: Girls' Community Schools (GCS) established (Number) Comments on achieving targets 387 GCS established benefiting 26,911 children enrolled as of Feb 2025. Number of GCS has declined to 382, but enrollment increased to 29,580 as of Sept 1, 2025.

risk_summary: Double-shift schools (DSS) have declined from 173 to 120 active schools, suggesting potential security, governance, or conflict-related challenges affecting the sustainability of alternative education delivery models in fragile districts.
evidence_quote: Government schools operating in double shifts (Number) Comments on achieving targets Achieved as of Feb 2025. Number of schools that have implemented a double-shift model (173 DSS achieved: 74 boys, 99 girls). Number of active DSS has declined over time to 120 as of September 1, 2025 (47 boys, 73 girls).
